title: Let the profile choose the optimization target
description: Optimize the function the profiler proves dominates cost, not the code that looks slow, so effort lands on the actual bottleneck.
body: Use when deciding where to spend optimization effort. Do not guess from intuition or micro-optimize a path that the profile shows is negligible, and avoid optimizing before measuring. Actions: capture a representative profile under realistic load, read the flame graph or top-N self/total time, and pick the dominant frame; estimate the ceiling (Amdahl) before investing. Record the profiled hotspot and its share of total time. Validation signals include a flame graph identifying the frame and a post-change profile showing the share dropped. Risk boundary: if the hottest frame is in a dependency or syscall you cannot change, escalate a design change rather than churning unreachable code.
